item.title: 11 Profitable App Ideas You can Build: Quickest Way to $10K+ per Month
item.description:
Many app developers are looking for profitable app ideas to take to the app store and generate over $10K per month. This article will discuss profitable app ideas that you could do today!

It takes to build an app that can generate over $300 per day. So, it is crucial to have app ideas that are profitable and easy to do. These app ideas will change your life if you take action today!
If you want a list of app ideas, then keep watching!

If you prefer video, here is the youtube version.

App Idea #01: Book Creator App

Book creator app offers users to create amazing books using text, images, and even videos. The app's interface should be very easy, making it perfect for easy publishing ebooks! After creating a book from this app, the user should easily publish it on the Amazon Kindle store.
This app idea can quickly increase your revenue by $300+ per day if you take action today!

App Idea #02: Real Estate calculator App

The real Estate app is all about helping real estate agents calculate the price of any property they are advertising. This app should be very easy to use for both agents and customers.

All you need to do is enter details like size, location, etc. Along with your preferences (like the number of bedrooms), this app will generate a fair price for you.

App Idea #03: Car Insurance Calculator App

A car insurance calculator app allows users to calculate their monthly or yearly premium depending on the type of vehicle they want to insure. This app will help users decide before taking out a policy. 

App Idea #04: Video Editing App

Video editing app allows users to edit their own videos using a very easy and intuitive interface. This app can be used by anyone, including people who are not tech-savvy!

The app should allow video trimming, resizing (changing aspect ratio), and adding filters like the old Instagram-style ones. The app also needs an option for uploading videos to YouTube or Vimeo.

App Idea #05: Music Creator App

Music app allows users to create their own music using a very easy and intuitive interface. This app is perfect for anyone who wants to make songs, beats, and melodies, but they do not know the first thing about how it works!
This app should allow sounds editing like cutting them into pieces or changing the pitch/tempo. The app will also need an option to upload your creations on Soundcloud (or any other similar platform) so that others can listen to your tunes.

App Idea #06: App Flipping Marketplace

App flipping app is for app developers who want to monetize their apps without publishing them on the app store. This app can be used by other people looking to buy an app that has already been published in the Play Store or App Store but was never updated after its release!

This marketplace could work like eBay. Where you will see a list of different applications available, the date when they were initially launched, and how much they're being sold for right now. As soon as someone buys your app, you get paid using PayPal instantly.

App Idea #07: Hire Virtual Assistant App

They hire a virtual assistant app for entrepreneurs looking to outsource their daily tasks. This app can be used by anyone in need of extra help with anything, especially if they're too busy or do not know how to get it done themselves!

This app will allow users to browse through an intuitive interface and a list of professional assistants already registered on the platform. The user then selects one based on various factors like qualifications/experience and schedules a call before hiring them immediately! 

App Idea #08: Simple App Builder

Simple app builder app is for entrepreneurs who want to build their own app but lack the technical skills or knowledge required. Anyone can use this app, including people who do not even have a business idea yet!

This app will allow users to choose from different apps like restaurants, dating websites, messaging services, etc. The user then selects their preference and any additional options before creating an app right away using the one-click installation feature! 

App Idea #09: Virtual Reality App Store

An app that allows people to buy and sell virtual reality content. This app is perfect for VR developers who can sell their videos, apps, and games directly to users through this app. 

This app will allow people to browse through a list of the different content available using an intuitive interface. The user then selects the ones they like, downloads them right away, and starts playing/experiencing virtual reality almost instantly!

App Idea #10: Language app

An app that teaches people a new language. There are many ways to monetize this app, like in-app purchases, video ads, etc.  

This app will allow people to choose the language they want to learn. Then it uses different techniques like videos, audio lessons, games, challenges, etc. to teach that person how to speak/understand other languages!

App Idea #11: App that allows people to make money using their phone

This app will let users do simple tasks on their mobile phones, such as watching videos or taking surveys. The user gets paid for completing these tasks and can instantly withdraw the money through PayPal/other similar services! 

App Idea #12: App that allows people to send messages anonymously (like Snapchat)

App, where users can anonymously message friends, etc. App, will delete messages after a certain time and user cannot send the same message to the same person, etc. app can also be used for one-on-one messaging.

This app is perfect for people who want to stay anonymous when communicating with others. The app allows them to send texts or pictures without revealing their identity so they can chat freely!

App Idea #13: App that allows people to play games with friends (like Words With Friends)

This app is perfect for app developers that want to make money through the app.
This app will allow people to play a variety of games with their friends and/or family members using an intuitive interface. The user can choose from various options like chatting during the game, challenge mode where they have to complete certain tasks in order to win, etc., all within this app!

The app can also be used for in-game purchases!
You can build this app quickly if you know what you are doing!!

App Idea #14: App where people can buy coupons (like Groupon)

An app where users can find restaurants, etc, and purchase coupons for discounts/deals on food, etc..

This app will allow people to browse through a list of different coupons and deals available in their location using an intuitive interface. The user can then choose which ones they like, share them with friends or family members, redeem them at the restaurant/store right away!

The app also has social features where users can add locations on the map so everyone knows what places are good near them. They can then create groups and invite others into these groups while communicating with each other about certain topics related to food etc..!!

App Idea #15: Local marketplace app

An app where users can post items they want to sell and get local buyers in their area.

This app will allow people to post items they want to sell and advertise them through different features like posting social updates, geo-tagging etc.
People who are searching for certain products can browse through this app using an intuitive interface and purchase any item right away or make a bid on it! This app is perfect for anyone selling their old stuff around the house!!

App Idea #16: App where people can order food online (like GrubHub/Seamless)

An app that allows users to find restaurants, etc, and buy food for delivery.
This app will allow users to find a restaurant/other food places based on their location and buy the food they want using an intuitive interface. The app can also be used for one-on-one communication between the buyer and seller so they can agree on an order, see the delivery status, etc.

App Idea #17: App where people can get paid to watch videos (like Swagbucks)

App where users can view ads and earn money.
This app will allow users to watch videos, ads, etc., and get paid for it by app developers or advertisers!

The user can then choose how they want to be paid using PayPal/other similar services so the app is perfect for anyone looking to make some extra money!!

App Idea #18: Digital Product Offer Wall app

An app that allows users to find and download digital products from different categories, and buy them. The app would be a mix of Pinterest, Evernote and Reddit wrapped into one app! This profitable app idea can also include affiliate links for each product so you could make money by promoting other people's products.

App Idea #19: On-demand Car Wash App

The on-demand car wash app allows customers to order services from home without having to leave their vehicle at any given time! This app has huge potential since everyone owns a car these days and no one wants to spend hours waiting for their vehicle to get washed.

All you need is a clean app that shows the location of your vehicle (car owners can mark where it's parked) along with all the information about getting it cleaned like photos, types of service options, etc. This app will allow users to schedule appointments in advance or simply order on-demand services whenever they want!

App Idea #20: Fitness App

A fitness app that allows you individuals or teams with similar workout schedules and habits to come together into groups called “teams” where members track each other’s progress by submitting what workouts they did during the day as well as how many reps were completed per exercise. This app also features a chat feature so that teammates could communicate more easily about exercises even though they might be in different locations.

This app will allow users to track workouts and share them with other people on the app, like friends or family members for example who want them to stay motivated!

App Idea #21: Transportation app

An app that allows users to schedule or track transportation services through a single platform. This app will allow users to book and pay for different types of transportation like cabs, Uber/Lyft-like services, buses, and trains in one place instead of using multiple apps!

App Idea #22: App where people can watch videos about how to do things (DIY)

An app that lets anyone post their own instructional video on anything they want so others can learn from it too! Whether you’re teaching someone how to bake the perfect chocolate cake or fix an old car this app is perfect for everyone since we all have something unique we could teach other people!!

This app would also feature social features like comments and likes so users can interact with each other and have discussions instead of just watching videos!

App Idea #23: School app

An app for students, teachers and parents that allows them to keep track of everything related to school life. The app will allow users to view grades, homework assignments, teacher contact information (for quick questions), student profiles so they can see photos/contact info if need be in case of an emergency or other important events happening at school etc., as well as the ability to communicate with all members on the app through a built-in chat feature!

This app has huge potential since it would benefit everyone involved in education like administrators who could use it too when looking into performance data instead of using multiple apps which is much more time-consuming.   

App Idea #24: On-demand Grocery Delivery Apps (like UberEats)

An app that allows users to order groceries from their favorite supermarkets and have them delivered at a specific time which they can schedule in advance, or simply request on-demand services. This app will allow grocery stores to make more revenue by selling products through the mobile app as well as reaching customers outside of store hours!

App Idea #25: Event app (like Eventbrite)    

An event app where individuals can search for events based on categories like music, sports, etc., buy tickets online using PayPal/credit card options, and then get notifications about updates related to the event such as location changes, cancellations etc., all within one platform so it's much easier than going back and forth between different websites trying to find out information!

The app will also include social media share buttons so users can invite their friends to the app and even promote events on social media channels like Instagram, Facebook, etc. for free advertising!      

This app would be perfect for event organizers since it's much easier than having them pay tons of money in fees by using other apps that charge big commissions or use affiliate marketing programs when they could just have a direct connection with customers through this app instead.

App Idea #26: Exercise Classes app

An exercise classes app where individuals can search for different types of workouts according to location, interest etc., book online without waiting in line at gyms/studios and then get notified about updates related to the class such as cancel if necessary which saves time too!

This app would be great for anyone who wants to sign up for a class in advance before going or opts out last minute since they can reschedule it.

App Idea #27: Watch Party App

An app for people who love to host parties and watch their favorite TV shows together. The app will allow users to invite friends/family members with one-touch access, chat about the event in real-time during the show they're watching or after it's over so everyone can discuss what happened, suggest future episodes that could be interesting, etc., among other features!

This app would be great for socialites since we all know how much of a hassle it is trying to get everyone together at the same place and having them RSVP late even when you send out invitations weeks beforehand which wastes so much time on our part!  

App Idea #28: Makeup app (like Sephora/Ulta)

An app that allows individuals to buy makeup based on product reviews, videos, tutorials, etc. Read about new releases, and sees exclusive offers for app users only. This app will also allow companies like MAC cosmetics to provide its customers with the ability to purchase products directly through the platform which saves time instead of checking out other websites or even waiting in line at physical stores!

This app would be perfect since it serves as an all-in-one shop where people can save their cart (so they don't forget what products they wanted), make quick purchases without having to wait online if necessary, and then get updates when items are shipped so there's no need to check back often just for updates which saves time too!

App Idea #29: Cleaning app

An app where users can request house cleaning services based on location, read reviews of cleaners, and then get notified when the cleaner is arriving. This app will allow anyone to hire a cleaner easily without having it being too expensive since there are no middlemen involved!

This app would be great for people who don't have time or prefer not to clean their homes themselves especially if they live alone or with just one other person so that means more free time for them instead of scrubbing toilets all day long!  

App Idea #30: Babysitter finder app

An app for parents with small children who want to find babysitters based on location, ratings/reviews, etc. Parents will also be able to schedule different types of events like playdates through this app which saves time instead of searching manually!

This app would be perfect for working individuals or anyone else who wants some extra cash by taking care of other people's kids while they're away whether it's a few hours or overnight depending on availability since you can make quick money without having to look elsewhere.

App Idea #31: Travel app (like Airbnb)    

An app that allows users to rent properties according to location, read reviews about the property and host/renter, get notified when someone books their place so there are no mistakes! This app will save time looking through countless listings which take forever especially if we want something last minute instead!

This app would be great for small business owners. For example, say user developer is traveling and needs a place to stay for the night while they're visiting another city. They can use this app so that it's easier than searching through Google or other websites since we don't have time to waste while we're busy!

App Idea #32: Pet Sitter app      

An app where all pet sitters are listed by location, read reviews about each one of them from past customers, see prices per visit/per hour, etc., send messages back and forth with potential candidates before making a final decision which saves us tons of time instead of having to call everyone individually looking for someone who is available in our area when necessary!

This app would be great especially if you have to leave your pet for a while since it's difficult to find someone on short notice.

Besides that the app would be great for individuals who are looking for different types of jobs that they can do even if the time is limited or just want to make some extra money by taking care of other people's pets!    

App Idea #33: Repair app

An app where users can request a repairman based on location, read reviews about them from past customers/users, etc., and get notified when someone is arriving to their home which saves time instead of having to call each individual company trying to figure out who would be the best fit.

This app would be perfect for homeowners or anyone else that needs something repaired like clothes dryers (since it's difficult finding one in such short notice), broken door handles, leaky faucets, etc., since we'll know ahead of time exactly who will show up!

App Idea #34: Music Festival app

An app where people can buy tickets for different music festivals all over the world by scrolling down through numerous listings, see who's playing when/where, find friends that are going to the same festival as us (if they've also downloaded app), etc., which saves time instead of having to go through tons of websites trying to figure out where we want to go and if there are any good artists performing.

This app would be great for individuals traveling or anyone else who loves attending festivals since it will take less effort than searching manually on multiple sites especially if you don't have much free time!      

App Idea #35: Job app

An app where users can apply for different types of jobs by scrolling down through numerous listings based on location, read reviews about each one from past employers/employees, get notified when someone accepts our application, etc., which saves time instead of having to go through countless websites/applications every day looking for something that may be a good fit!

This app would be great especially after college since it's the perfect way to get our name out there and start networking with other professionals in different industries whether you're still searching for your dream job or need some extra cash on the side.  

App Idea #36: Drone app

An app where users can see pictures taken from drones based on location (i.e. New York City), read reviews about each drone by past customers/users,etc., so we know what we're paying for! This will save tons of time researching numerous companies & prices when all this information is conveniently in one app.

This app would be great especially for individuals who are into photography/videography since they can get amazing pictures that were taken from high up!    

App Idea #37: Date app

An app where users can see different singles in their area by scrolling down through numerous listings, read reviews about them from past girlfriends/boyfriends, and more! This saves tons of time instead of having to call each one individually or going on traditional dates.

This app would be great especially for individuals who are looking for serious relationships since it makes online dating extremely easy and convenient...no more wasting hours talking with people we don't find attractive or interesting which is a common problem when meeting someone organically!    

App Idea #38: Classic car app

An app where users can search classic cars based on location, read reviews about them from past customers/users, etc., which saves time instead of having to call each individual company trying to figure out who would be the best fit.

This app would be perfect for those looking into classic cars since we'll know exactly what they're getting and how much it's going to cost!    

App Idea #39: Pet app

An app where users can search different types of pets based on location, choose a breed that they want, see detailed information about how much food & toys will run us monthly as well as any health problems associated with owning this specific type of pet, compare prices between multiple vets in our area so we always go with the cheapest option without sacrificing quality of services, etc., which saves us a ton of time trying to figure out all this information on our own!

This app allows pet owners to make better decisions when it comes caring for their furry friend.

This app would be perfect especially if we're looking into getting a new cat or dog since there is so much that goes into owning one and most people don't realize what they'll have to pay until after the fact.    

App Idea #40: Self-Defense app

An app where users can choose from different self-defense classes based on location/type (i.e Kung Fu, Krav Maga), read reviews about each class by past students & teachers, see descriptions with pictures before signing up to determine which best suits them, etc., so we know what we're getting into before dropping money on something that isn't right for us.

This app would be great especially since it's an extremely popular topic nowadays!    

App Idea #41: Side Hustle app 

An app where users can search different side hustles (i.e driving for Uber), read reviews about each job from past employers/employees, get notified when someone accepts our application, etc., which saves tons of time instead of having to go through countless websites/applications every day looking for something that may be a good fit.

This app would be great for those who need a side hustle to make extra money or even full-time income, especially since the app will do all the work that normally takes up hundreds of hours!    

App Idea #42: Save app

An app where users can save money for a specific goal, read reviews about each company from past customers/users, purchase items at the best price possible so we never overpay again, etc., which saves us tons of time instead of calling individual businesses or even going to numerous stores looking for good deals. This app allows those who are trying to save money, and be more efficient with their shopping experiences!

This app would be perfect especially since it's extremely popular lately and most people struggle with saving as much as they want.    

App Idea #43: Allergy app 

An app that tracks our symptoms throughout the day & night depending on what is going around in our area based on pollen count, alerts us when we need to take medicine or head inside for our own safety, etc., so we don't put ourselves at risk by going outside with allergies.

This app would be great especially since it saves us from putting ourselves in harm's way and not knowing what is happening!    

App Idea #44: Yoga app 

An app where users can search different types of yoga classes based on location, read reviews about each session from past students/teachers, see which teacher has the best rating and why others enjoy their class so much, etc., so we know what to expect before signing up. This app allows those who love doing yoga but don't have a lot of time to be more efficient with our schedules by finding an instructor that works well for us!

This app would be perfect since it's extremely popular nowadays and people struggle trying to find a studio or teacher close by without wasting tons of money & time going through every single one out there!    

App Idea #45: Wedding app

This app would be perfect especially since it's extremely popular nowadays and most people struggle trying to find an app or broker without wasting tons of money & time going through every single one out there!    

App Idea #48: Alternative Medicine app  

An app where users can track how they're feeling throughout the day/week/month, read reviews about different alternative medicine treatments from past patients, see which ones work best for certain symptoms depending on what we have been experiencing recently, etc., so we know exactly how well something works before spending huge amounts on treatment that may not even work for us!
 

App Idea #49: Hiking app  

An app where users can search the best trails near them based on difficulty, read reviews about each trail from past hikers/climbers to see if it's worth going out of our way for, etc., so we don't waste time exploring every single one. This app allows those who love hiking but don't have a lot of time go through this huge list and find something they'll enjoy!

This app would be perfect especially since hiking is extremely popular nowadays and people struggle to try to find an activity that works well with their schedule without wasting tons of money & time going through every single one out there!    

App Idea #50: Skincare app 

An app where users can read reviews about every product, learn how to apply each one, track their skincare routine progress (i.e see which products are working best for your skin type), etc., so we don't waste money on something that won't work well or even hurt our skin! This app allows those who struggle with finding the right product to spend less time & energy trying different things to figure out what works best for them!

This app would be perfect especially since there's a huge demand for it and most people can't afford expensive treatments to fix everything. They also waste tons of time going through every single company/product out there without anyone recommending anything specific because everyone has different needs!

They also waste tons of time going through every single company/rental out there without anyone recommending anything specific because everyone has different needs!

item.title: Fullstack Ethereum Development Course
item.description:
Abdou Ouahib

Posted on Mar 14

TLDR: Check out this playlist to learn how to build a Full-stack Ethereum dApp.

Hi everyone👋

Web3/blockchain is one of the hottest topics right now, and a lot of developers want to get into this field. So if you're one of them, then keep reading.

I made a YouTube series where I build NFT Marketplace from scratch, and here are the steps I followed (what you will learn):

  1. Write the Ethereum smart contract using Solidity.
  2. Write integration tests for the smart contract, with the help of Hardhat.
  3. Deploy the smart contract to Rinkeby using Hardhat.
  4. Create and deploy a subgraph (The Graph protocol) to index the smart contract.
  5. Build a front-end using Next.js and Tailwindcss:
    • Use ethers.js to send Ethereum transactions (Create NFT, list NFT for sale, cancel NFT sale, buy NFT).
    • Use Apollo to query the subgraph GraphQL API endpoint and fetch NFT data.

The Tech Stack:

  1. Solidity for writing the smart contract.
  2. Hardhat as the Ethereum Development Environment.
  3. Typescript, Next.js and Tailwindcss for the front-end.
  4. Ethers.js for interacting with the blockchain from the front-end.
  5. The Graph protocol for indexing the smart contract.
  6. Apollo, both for querying the GraphQL endpoint, and as a CLI to generate Typescript interfaces for our queries.

The videos are heavily technical (lots of code), but I still explain everything I do, in detail.

Check out this playlist to start learning.
Here is the project repo.
And here's a hosted version of the dApp.

Happy coding!😇

Łukasz Czyszczonik - Feb 16

Suraj Vishwakarma - Mar 10

item.title: Release News! Apache DolphinScheduler 2.0.5 Optimizes The Fault Tolerance Process of Worker
item.description:
Recently, Apache DolphinScheduler announced the official release of version 2.0.5. This version has carried out some functional optimizations, such as optimizing the fault tolerance process of Worker, adding the function of re-uploading files in the resource center, and making several bug fixes.

Optimization

Worker fault tolerance process

Version 2.0.5 optimizes the worker's fault tolerance proc

ess so that when the server is interrupted due to excessive pressure, it can normally transfer tasks to other workers to continue execution to avoid task interruption.

Forbid to run task page sign optimization

Optimized the display of flags on pages where tasks are prohibited from running, which distinguishes from the display of tasks that are normally executed, to prevent users from confusing work status.

Added prompts to the task box

In version 2.0.5, a prompt is added to the task box to display all the long task names, which is convenient for users.


Added the re-uploading files function in the resource center

The function of re-uploading files has been added to the resource center. When the user needs to modify the execution, automatical updating of the execution script can be realized without the requirement to reconfigure the task parameters.

Jump to the list page directly when modifying the workflow

Changed the status that the page remained on the DAG page after modifying the workflow. After optimization, it can jump to the list page, which is convenient for users to follow-up operations.

Markdown information type added to DingTalk alert plugin

Adds the Markdown information type to the alarm content of the DingTalk alarm plugin to enrich the information type support.

Bug Fix

[#8213] The task run incorrectly when the worker group contains uppercase letters.

[#8347] Fixed the problem of workflow cannot be stopped when the task fails and retries

[#8135] JDBC connection parameter cannot input '@'

[#8367] Complement may not end normally

[#8170] Fix the problem of failing to enter the sub-workflow from the page

2.0.5 Download address:

https://dolphinscheduler.apache.org/en-us/download/download.html

Release Note: https://github.com/apache/dolphinscheduler/releases/tag/2.0.5

Thanks to Contributors

Thanks to the contributors of Apache DolphinScheduler 2.0.5 version, the list of contributor GitHub IDs is as follows (in no particular order):

Join the Community

There are many ways to participate and contribute to the DolphinScheduler community, including:

Documents, translation, Q&A, tests, codes, articles, keynote speeches, etc.

We assume the first PR (document, code) to contribute to be simple and should be used to familiarize yourself with the submission process and community collaboration style.

item.title: Configuring check_oracle Nagios plugin
item.description:
Fairywen

Posted on Mar 14

Let's see how to set-up Oracle database monitoring on an eyesofnetwork server, using check_oracle plugin.

Step 1 : Get check_oracle pluging

First, check_oracle plugin can already be available on Nagios server, look for yum package nagios-plugins-oracle.x86_64 : Nagios Plugin - check_oracle.
Install it if it's not.

Step 2 : Install oracle client

The lightest way to get all oracle dependancies we need is to :

  1. install an oracle-instantclient
  2. add tnsping by getting it on another server

Note : We also could have installed "full" oracle client, but I don't think it would have been relevant.

Step 2.1 : Get instant client

So let's get .rpm package on oracle instant client download page.

In this example we pick up version 19.14.0.0.0 in Basic Package (RPM) (wich contains SQL*Plus binary).

Once this package is pushed to targer server, install it :

[root@nagios-server ~]# yum install <path_to>/oracle-instantclient19.14-basic-19.14.0.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm
Modules complémentaires chargés : fastestmirror
Examen de oracle-instantclient19.14-basic-19.14.0.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm : oracle-instantclient19.14-basic-19.14.0.0.0-1.x86_64
Sélection de oracle-instantclient19.14-basic-19.14.0.0.0-1.x86_64.rpm pour installation
Résolution des dépendances
--> Lancement de la transaction de test
---> Le paquet oracle-instantclient19.14-basic.x86_64 0:19.14.0.0.0-1 sera installé
--> Résolution des dépendances terminée

Dépendances résolues ================================================================================================================================================================================================================================ Package Architecture Version Dépôt Taille
================================================================================================================================================================================================================================
Installation : oracle-instantclient19.14-basic x86_64 19.14.0.0.0-1 /oracle-instantclient19.14-basic-19.14.0.0.0-1.x86_64 226 M Résumé de la transaction
================================================================================================================================================================================================================================
Installation 1 Paquet Taille totale  : 226 M
Taille d'installation : 226 M
Is this ok [y/d/N]: y
Downloading packages:
Running transaction check
Running transaction test
Transaction test succeeded
Running transaction Installation : oracle-instantclient19.14-basic-19.14.0.0.0-1.x86_64 1/1 Vérification : oracle-instantclient19.14-basic-19.14.0.0.0-1.x86_64 1/1 Installé : oracle-instantclient19.14-basic.x86_64 0:19.14.0.0.0-1 Terminé !

At the end of installation, Oracle client is installed under /usr/lib/oracle/19.14/client64. That will be our ORACLE_HOME directory for next steps.

Step 2.2 Add tnsping

To do so we just have to get $ORACLE_HOME/bin/tnsping and $ORACLE_HOME/network/mesg/tnsus.msb from database server (also running on linux distribution), copy it to Nagios server in equivalent directories and check file permissions.

[root@nagios-server ~]# echo $ORACLE_HOME
/usr/lib/oracle/19.14/client64
[root@nagios-server ~]# mv tnsping $ORACLE_HOME/bin/
[root@nagios-server ~]# cd $ORACLE_HOME/bin/
[root@nagios-server bin]# ls -la
total 180
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 11 mars 10:42 .
drwxr-xr-x 5 root root 4096 11 mars 10:12 ..
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 41504 25 déc. 14:20 adrci
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 59032 25 déc. 14:20 genezi
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 24512 25 déc. 14:22 sqlplus
-rw-rw-r-- 1 user user 42216 11 mars 10:34 tnsping
[root@nagios-server bin]# chmod 755 tnsping
[root@nagios-server bin]# chown root.root tnsping
[root@nagios-server bin]# ls -la
total 180
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 11 mars 10:42 .
drwxr-xr-x 5 root root 4096 11 mars 10:12 ..
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 41504 25 déc. 14:20 adrci
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 59032 25 déc. 14:20 genezi
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 24512 25 déc. 14:22 sqlplus
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 42216 11 mars 10:34 tnsping

[root@nagios-server ~]# mkdir $ORACLE_HOME/network/mesg
[root@nagios-server ~]# mv tnsus.msb $ORACLE_HOME/network/mesg/.
[root@nagios-server ~]# cd $ORACLE_HOME/network/mesg
[root@nagios-server mesg]# ls -la
total 60
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 11 mars 10:51 .
drwxr-xr-x 4 root root 4096 11 mars 10:50 ..
-rw-rw-r-- 1 user user 50688 11 mars 10:49 tnsus.msb
[root@nagios-server mesg]# chown root.root tnsus.msb
[root@nagios-server mesg]# chmod 644 tnsus.msb
[root@nagios-server mesg]# ls -la
total 60
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 11 mars 10:51 .
drwxr-xr-x 4 root root 4096 11 mars 10:50 ..
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 50688 11 mars 10:49 tnsus.msb

Step 3 : add tnsnames file

By default after installation, network/admin directory is under /usr/lib/oracle/19.14/client64/lib. Usually this would be under $ORACLE_HOME, so let's move it :

[root@nagios-server ~]# cd /usr/lib/oracle/19.14/client64/
[root@nagios-server client64]# mv lib/network .

Then we can create an usual tnsnames.ora under $ORACLE_HOME/network/admin/ looking like this :

MY_DB = (DESCRIPTION = (ADDRESS = (PROTOCOL = <protocol>)(HOST = <ip>)(PORT = <port>)) (CONNECT_DATA = (SID = ORA_SID) ) )

Step 4 : Configure check command

As explained in this Nagios support topic, Nagios plugins doesn't have access to environment variables when running, thus there is no point to set ORACLE_HOME or update LD_LIBRARY_PATH in linux system.
Instead, we have to set them directly in check command definition in /etc/nagios/objects/commands/commands.cfg, example for a tns check :

define command {
  command_name my_check_oracle_tns
  command_line /usr/bin/env LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/usr/lib/oracle/19.14/client64/lib ORACLE_HOME=/usr/lib/oracle/19.14/client64 /srv/eyesofnetwork/nagios/plugins/check_oracle --tns $ARG1$
}

That's all, our Nagios server have now a new command to monitor Oracle database server 😃

item.title: Building a responsive website? Start with mobile first!
item.description:
When designing a new responsive layout, which one do you do first, desktop or mobile? Here, I'll show you why it's best to develop the mobile version first and desktop second.

Two main reasons:

1. Start simple, enhance gradually

Take a look at the two layouts below, the left is mobile and the right is desktop:

Which one of the two looks easier to build?

The mobile follows the "default" layout applied by all browsers without any special CSS, which is the "flow layout". So to keep things simple, start with the mobile layout and enhance to a grid for screen sizes that allow it:


#gallery{ /* enter basic CSS to style the gallery here */
} @media (min-width: 600px){ #gallery{ display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); }
}

2. More people will visit your site on a phone than on a desktop

More and more people visit the Internet using their mobile devices, and there's a good chance that the same is true for your website.

Designing for mobile first, means you are developing for the growing majority of your visitors. You can later enhance for your desktop audience from that point on.

Thanks for reading. Do you build responsive layouts mobile first or desktop first? Why?

item.title: What is NodeJS?
item.description:
artemismars

Posted on Mar 14

I've been studying nodejs half a year but I was just implementing APIs without understanding the foundation of tools I use.
I decided to write this post to check the foundation stuff of NodeJS! :)

NodeJS is an runtime environment that is built on V8 engine ( which compiles javascript codes into native machine codes).

NodeJS has some special features like event-driven, non-blocking(asynchronous) programming.

  1. What is Event driven programming? > Event driven programming in NodeJS means that events occurred by mouse and network communication like request and response are handled via callback functions. here's some example.

Mouse event

const card = document.getElementById('card');
canvas.addEventListener('click', eventHandler);

Network event

app.get('/', (req, res) => { res.send("hello world");
});

You can see that those events are handled through callback functions. Events are processed by Event Loop. and the Event loop processes the Phases and these Phases have Queues. Event loops

will choose phases with Round Robin scheduling method.

A round robin is an arrangement of choosing all elements in a group equally in some rational order, usually from the top to the bottom of a list and then starting again at the top of the list and so on.

  1. Non-Blocking
    NodeJS supports a single thread programming. This means every process in NodeJS lives in a thread. But since it's single thread if the first process is working long time then next processes should wait until the first process ends. But NodeJS is Non-Blocking. So, Even tho the first process didn't end yet, next processes can be executed. This will make your app still working even tho there's heavy data being processed.

  2. Cross-Platform runtime environment.
    Your Nodejs codes will work on different operating systems like OSX, MacOS, Windows!

If you have some advice, please share it via comments!
Thanks for reading my post!
item.author: artemismars

item.title: Big O notation explained: DSA-01
item.description:
Big O notation is used to measure how the running time or space requirements for our program grows as input grows.

  • Measuring running time growth is time complexity. (In this post we focus on time complexity)
  • Measuring space growth is space complexity.

Rules to determine time complexity

  1. Keep fastest growing term.
  2. Drop constants.

Big O refers to very large value of 'n' (n = size) where time, t = an^2 + bn + c.

For example, if we have a function like,

t = 5n^2 + 3n + 20

When value of 'n' is very large, bn+c becomes irrelevant. ie; an^2 is very larger than bn+c. For example; if n = 1000 then,

t = 5 * 1000^2 + 3*1000+20 = 5000000 + 3020; where 3020, a small value becomes irrelevant.

Different time complexities

Here we discuss about a few of them.

1. O(n)

def foo(arr): size(arr) -> 100 -> 0.22ms

def foo(arr): size(arr) -> 1000 -> 2.30ms

Here time increases as array size increases; time proportional to size(arr). n = size(arr), b= constant

t = a*n + b

  1. Keep fastest growing term => t = a * n (b is constant)
  2. Drop constants => t = n ( a is constant) Therefore; t = O(n)

Example program for t = O(n): To get square numbers

def get_squared_numbers(numbers):
    squared_numbers = []
    for n in numbers:
        square_numbers.append(n * n)
    return squared_numbers

numbers = [2, 5, 8, 9]
get_squared_numbers(numbers)
# returns [4, 25, 64, 81]

2. O(1)

def foo(arr): size(arr) -> 100 -> 0.22ms

def foo(arr): size(arr) -> 1000 -> 0.23ms

time, t = a * n + b -> t = a * n ( b constant) -> t = n (dropping constants(a)) n = 1 => t = 1

Therefore, t = O(1)

Example program for t = O(1)

def find_first_pe(prices, eps, index):
    pe = prices[index]/eps[index]
    return pe

3. O(n^2)

Example Program for O(n^2): To find duplicates from the list

numbers = [3, 6, 2, 4, 3, 6, 8, 9]

for i in range(len(numbers)):
    for j in range(i + 1, len(numbers)):
        if numbers[i] == numbers[j]:
            print(numbers[i] + " is a duplicate.")
            break

Also there is O(log n), O(2^n) time complexities.

item.title: Backup your files to S3, Google Drive, .etc using Rclone
item.description:
Mochammad Rafi

Posted on Mar 14

Rclone is a command line program to manage files on cloud storage. Over 40 cloud storage products support rclone including S3, Google Drive, and more.

To install rclone on Linux/macOS/BSD systems, run:

curl https://rclone.org/install.sh | sudo bash

For Windows and other formats can be downloaded at https://rclone.org/downloads/

How to use

First you need to create a config using the rclone config command.

After that do sync files using the command rclone sync source:path dest:path [flags], an example is rclone sync /backup-folder google-drive:folder-name.

Additional commands for displaying upload progress using -P or other commands can be found at https://rclone.org/commands/.

Basically you can make automatic backups using cron jobs or schedule tasks.

Thank you for reading this article, keep your files safe using backup👾

item.author: mochammadrafi

item.title: Exploration&Practice of Tujia Big Data Platform Based on Apache DolphinScheduler
item.description:
Tujia introduced Apache DolphinScheduler in 2019. At the recent Apache DolphinScheduler Meetup in February, Tujia Big Data Engineer Xuchao Zan introduced the process of Tujia's access to Apache DolphinScheduler and the functional improvements in detail.


Xuchao Zan, Big Data Engineer, Data Development engineer from Tujia, is mainly responsible for the development, maintenance, and tuning of the big data platform.

Watch the record here:https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV1Ki4y117WV?spm_id_from=333.999.0.0

This speech mainly consists of 4 parts. The first part is the current status of Tujia's platform, introducing the process of Tujia's data flow, how Tujia provides data services, and the role of Apache DolphinScheduler in the platform. The second part will introduce the scheduler selection process of Tujia, which mainly refers to some features of the scheduler and the process of access. The third part is mainly about some improvements and functional expansions of the system, including support for function table dependencies, mail tasks expansion, and data synchronization functions. The fourth part details some new functions we have developed to meet our business requirements, such as reforming the Spark jar packages to support the publishing system, connecting the scheduler to data quality, and displaying the Data Lineage.

Status of Tujia Big Data Platform

01 Big Data Platform schema

First, let's introduce the schema of Tujia Big Data Platform and the role of Apache DolphinScheduler in the platform.

The architecture of Tujia Big Data Platform

The picture above shows the architecture of our data platform, which mainly includes data source, data collection, data storage, data management, and finally service provision.

The main source of the data includes three parts: data synchronization of the business from library MySQL API, involving the Dubbo interface, the http interface, and the embedded point data of the web page.

Data collection adopts real-time and offline synchronization, business data is incremental synchronized based on Canal, logs are collected by Flume, and Kafka is collected in real-time and falls on HDFS.

The data storage process mainly involves some data synchronization services. After the data falls into HDFS, it is cleaned, processed, and then pushed online to provide services.

At the data management level, the data dictionary records the metadata information of the business, the definition of the model, and the mapping relationship between various levels, which is convenient for users to find the data they care about; the log records the operation log of the task, and the alarm configures the fault information, etc. The dispatching system, as a command center of big data, rationally allocates dispatching resources and can better serve the business. Metrics library records the dimensions and attributes, normative definitions of business process metrics for better management and usage of data. Abtest documents the impact of different metrics and strategies on product functionality, and data quality is the basis for the effectiveness and accuracy of data analysis.

The last part is the*data service*, which mainly includes ad hoc data query, report preview, data download and upload analysis, online business data support release, etc.

02 The role of Apache DolphinScheduler in the platform

The following focuses on the role of the scheduler in the platform. The data tunnel is synchronized, and incremental data is pulled regularly every morning. After the data is cleaned and processed, it is pushed online to provide services. It also charges the processing of the data model and the interface configuration greatly improves Productivity. The service of the timed report, push email, support the display of attachments, text table, and line chart. The report push function allows the analysts to configure data dashboards, and  DataX pushes the calculated data to MySQL every day for report display after data processing.

Introduces Apache DolphinScheduler

The second part is about the work we have done by introducing Apache DolphinScheduler.

Apache DolphinScheduler is advanced in many aspects. As a command center of big data, it's undoubtedly reliable. The decentralized design of Apache DolphinScheduler avoids the single point of failure problem, and once a problem occurs with one node, the task will be automatically restarted on other nodes, which greatly improves the system's reliability.

In addition, Apache DolphinScheduler is simple and practical, which reduces learning costs and improves work efficiency. Now many staff in the company are using it, including analysts, product operational staff, and developers.

The scalability of scheduling is also very important because as the amount of tasks increases, the cluster can add resources in time to provide services. A wide range of applications is also a key reason for us to choose Apache DolphinScheduler. It supports a variety of task types: Shell, MR, Spark, SQL (MySQL, PostgreSQL, Hive, SparkSQL), Python, Sub_Process, Procedure, etc. and enables workflow timing scheduling and dependency scheduling, manual scheduling, manual pause/stop/resume, as well as retry/alarm on failure, recovery from specified node failure, killing tasks, etc. It has so many advantages, and I cannot list them one by one, you should try it by yourself.

Next is the upgrade of our time scheduling.

Before adopting Apache DolphinScheduler, our scheduling was quite confusing. Some people deployed their own local Crontab, some used Oozie for scheduling, and some used the system for time schedules. The management is chaotic, the timeliness& accuracy cannot be guaranteed, and tasks cannot be found from time to time due to lacking one unified scheduling platform. In addition, the self-built scheduler is not stable enough due to lacking configuration dependency and data output guarantee, and the product function is limited, which supports limited task scheduling.

In 2019, we introduced Apache DolphinScheduler, and it has been running stably for nearly 3  years.

Below is some data of our system transfer.

We have built an Apache DolphinScheduler cluster with a total of 4 physical machines. Currently, a single machine supports 100 task scheduling concurrently.

Algorithms are also configured with special machines and be isolated.

Most of our tasks are processed by Oozie, which are mainly Spark and Hive tasks. There are also some scripts on Crontab, some mailing tasks, and scheduled tasks of the reporting system.

The Scheduling System Re-built Based on Apache DolphinScheduler

Before reconstruction, we have optimized the system, such as supporting table-level dependencies, extending the mail function, etc. After introducing Apache DolphinScheduler, we re-built a scheduling system based on it to provide better services.

Firstly, the synchronization of table dependencies was supported.At that time, considering the task migration may be parallel, the tasks could not be synchronized all at once and needed to be marked as the table tasks run successfully. Therefore, we developed the function to solve the dependency problem in task migration. However, the different naming style of users makes it difficult to locate the task of the table when configuring dependencies, and we cannot identify which tables are included in the task, and where the task is located in the table, which causes a lot of trouble for us. 

Secondly, Mail tasks support multiple tables.Scheduling has its self-installed mail push function, but only supports a single table. With more and more business requirements, we need to configure multiple tables and multiple sheets, and the number of text and attachments required to be displayed differently, which needs to be configured. In addition, it is necessary to support the function of line charts to enrich the text pages. Furthermore, users also want to be able to add notes to the text or below each table to explain indicators, etc. We use the Spark jar package to implement the email push function, which supports abnormal warnings, table dependencies missing, etc.


Thirdly, it supports rich data source synchronization. Due to some data transmission issues, we needed to modify configuration codes greatly, compile, package and upload data in the previous migration process, which is complex and error-prone, and data and online data cannot be separated due to data source disunity; in terms of development efficiency, there is a lot of repetition in the code, and the lack of a unified configuration tool and the unreasonable parameter configuration lead to high pressure on MySQL and the risk of downtime; after data transmission, there is no duplicate check, when the amount of data is large, the full amount of updates will cause a lot of pressure on MySQL. MySQL's transmission has a single point of failure problem, and task delay affects online services.


We simplified the data development process, made MySQL support the high availability of pxc/mha, and improved the efficiency of data synchronization.

The input data sources support relational databases and FTP synchronization. As the computing engine, the output data sources of Spark support various relational databases, as well as message middleware Kafka, MQ, and Redis.

Next, let's get through the process of our implementation.

We have extended the data source of Apache DolphinScheduler to support the expansion of Kafka mq and namespace. Before MySQL synchronization, we first calculate an increment locally and synchronize the incremental data to MySQL. Spark also supports the HA of MySQL pxc/qmha. In addition, there is a qps limit when pushing MQ and Redis, we control the number of partitions and concurrency of Spark by the amount of data.

Improvements

The fourth part is mainly about the added functions to the system, including:

  1. Spark supports publishing system
  2. Data quality open up
  3. Display of Data lineage ### 01 Spark task supports publishing system

More than 80% of our usual schedules are Spark jar package tasks, but the task release process and the code modification are not normalized. This leads to code inconsistencies from time to time, and even causes online problems in severe cases.

This requires us to refine the release process for tasks. We mainly use the publishing system, the Jenkens packaging function, compile and package to generate btag, and then publish and generate rtag after the test is completed, and the code is merged into master. This avoids the problem of code inconsistency and reduces the steps for jar package upload. After compiling and generating the jar package, the system will automatically push the jar package to the resource center of Apache DolphinScheduler. Users only need to configure the parameters and select the jar package for test release. When running a Spark task, it is no longer necessary to pull the file to the local, but directly read the jar package on HDFS.

02 Data quality connection

Data quality is the basis for ensuring the validity and accuracy of analytical conclusions. We need a complete data monitoring output process to make the data more convincing. The quality platform ensures data accuracy, integrity, consistency, and timeliness from four aspects, and supports multiple alarm methods such as telephone, WeCom, and email to inform users.

Next, we will introduce how to connect the data quality and scheduling system. After the scheduling task is completed, the message record is sent, the data quality platform receives the message, and triggers the rule of the data quality monitoring. Abide by the monitoring rule, the downstream operation is blocked or an alarm message is sent.

03 Data linage display

Data lineage is key to metadata management, data governance, and data quality. It can track the source, processing, and provenance of data, provide a basis for data value assessment, and describe the flow of source data among processes, tables, reports, and ad hoc queries, the dependencies between tables, tables & offline ETL tasks, as well as scheduling platforms & computing engines. The data warehouse is built on Hive, and the raw data of Hive often comes from the production DB, and the calculation results are also exported to external storage. Therefore, the tables of heterogeneous data sources are related.

  • Data trace: When data is abnormal, it helps to trace the cause of the abnormality; impact analysis, tracing the source of the data and data processing process.
  • Data evaluation: Provide a basis for evaluating data value in terms of data target, update importance, and update frequency.
  • Life cycle:Intuitively obtain the entire life cycle of data, providing a basis for data governance. The collection process of data linage is mainly about: Spark monitors the SQL and inserted tables by monitoring the Spark API, obtains and parses the Spark execution plan.

That's it for my talk, thank you all!

Join the Community

There are many ways to participate and contribute to the DolphinScheduler community, including:

Documents, translation, Q&A, tests, codes, articles, keynote speeches, etc.

We assume the first PR (document, code) to contribute to be simple and should be used to familiarize yourself with the submission process and community collaboration style.

Your Star for the project is important, don’t hesitate to lighten a Star for Apache DolphinScheduler ❤️
item.author: dolphinschedule

item.title: Outsourcing Web Development: Misconceptions & Facts
item.description:
When it comes to web development, there has been a hot debate in leading companies regarding whether to get an in-house team or just outsource web development services.

The trend says more and more leading companies are leaning towards outsourcing the expert development team, but then there are few concerns about the quality work and insufficient involvement of the outsourced team.

On the other hand, the higher cost of the in-house team can limit your project in multiple ways.

Having said that, let me draw your attention to several misconceptions that exist even in the leading companies doing business for many years.

Even today, in modern tech-enhanced times, many prejudices and misconceptions block them from getting outstanding services with fewer resources involved.

In this blog, I will be shading light on some facts regarding outsourcing web development services. I will try to address the prominent myths and misconceptions that are pretty common in the industry.

First of all, let's understand what exactly outsourcing web development is and how it works.

Software Outsourcing is nothing but having an external source fulfil your Web Development requirements rather than hiring and building an in-house team.

This was the basic idea of outsourcing web development services. However, things can get more complicated with the different types of services offered by various outsourcing web development companies. Mainly there are three types of outsourcing- onshore, nearshore, and offshore web development services.

Now, let's address the common myths that revolve around outsourcing web development.

Myth 1. Outsourcing is only for big-shot businesses!

The trend of outsourcing started in the last decade of the 20th century. But since the arrival of the offshore outsourcing concept, more and more players have joined the race observing the rapid growth of this industry.

If I quote the basic principle of supply and demand, due to more suppliers of web development services, the contract rates have been decreasing consistently for a significant time.

Now the situation has reached that point where even beginners or small enterprises can afford web development outsourcing regardless of the project size.

So, it would be safe to say that outsourcing web development is not just for hotshot companies, but it's a suitable model for every kind of business entity.

Myth 2. A nightmare of an intercultural communication

This belief, especially in non-development companies, has been that often communication between intercultural team members can be a tough nut to crack.

The difficulty happens in the special cases of transcontinental outsourcing experience. However, this is not completely true. Certain companies in Asian countries and some European companies successfully cater to the web development requirements of global companies.

The secret ingredient of this successful and, most importantly, performance-oriented outsourcing experience is the working culture followed by the outsourcing web development companies.

Most leading outsourcing companies follow Agile or the Waterfall methodology to maintain transparency and diligence in their workflow.

Myth 3. One Bad attempt, and it's all over!

If your first attempt gets you a bitter taste of failure while outsourcing the development services, that does not mean it will get you another failure next time as well.

Sure, the first bad attempt has put you in the middle of some mediocre talent. You have some trust issues, but taking care of certain attributes before hiring a web development company for your next project can save you valuable resources from wasted time.

To get a profound experience in the next attempt, ensure the company follows a transparent work culture, preferably Agile Methodology. Furthermore, take the precise idea of the developer's expertise who is going to be involved in your project before making any commitments.

Make sure the developer team comprehends your vision of the project; hence, it would be feasible to be on the same page and reach the goals without any hurdles.

Myth 4. Not Relying on anybody, only I can do the best

Generally, when the company is new and finds its way to being successful, founders do all the work from A to Z. Therefore, they believe that things would not turn out perfect if they have not done it.

But once the company sees its first success and starts growing, you will not be able to do everything with your hands. You would need to acquire experts to do things that you would not be able to.

The key here is to have trust and give liberty to make creative changes by the developer team you are outsourcing.

Myth 5. Outsourcers might steal my data

The concern of theft is quite rational as everyone should have this concern while allowing a third party to your project with your resources.

But, if you look at it the other way. The gain from stealing and selling the data for personal gain is there, but on another hand, the reputational harm for such data leak can cause a lot more harm than the gain from the data.

Therefore, it's unlikely that any reputed web development service provider can act on such short-term profit.

I hope I have burst the bubbles of these myths. However, we tend to fall through other slippery slopes in the outsourcing web development world without even knowing.

To avoid such misconceptions and to outsource a top-notch web development company, check out this in-depth guide on Web Development Outsourcing beforehand.

Apart from that, if you still have doubts about outsourcing experience for web development services, especially for backend Development, you can approach and Hire RoR Developer from one of the result-oriented outsourcing web development companies.
item.author: arohi

item.title: Pagination with Django and Bootstrap 5
item.description:
Brylie Christopher Oxley

Posted on Mar 14

In this recipe, we will use Django pagination to display a paginated list of Items and add Bootstrap 5 styles. Replace the Item class with any Django model in your own project.

Create the paginated list

In the View, such as in the get_context method of a class-based view:

# Add these imports at the top of your View file
from django.core.paginator import ( Paginator, EmptyPage, PageNotAnInteger,
) # Then, in the view, such as the get_context method:

# Get page number from request, # default to first page
page = request.GET.get('page', 1) # Get queryset of items to paginate
items = Item.objects.all() # Paginate items
items_per_page = 10
paginator = Paginator(items, items_per_page) try: items_page = paginator.page(page)
except PageNotAnInteger: items_page = paginator.page(1)
except EmptyPage: items_page = paginator.page(paginator.num_pages) # Provide filtered, paginated library items
context["items_page"] = items_page

Render the paginated items and paginator widget

Once you have passed the paginated items to the template, render the items along with a paginated button group.

{% if items_page %} {% for item in items_page %} {% include "items/item_template.html" %} {% endfor %} {% if items_page.has_other_pages %} <div class="btn-group" role="group" aria-label="Item pagination"> {% if items_page.has_previous %} <a href="?page={{ items_page.previous_page_number }}" class="btn btn-outline-primary">«</a> {% endif %} {% for page_number in items_page.paginator.page_range %} {% if items_page.number == page_number %} <button class="btn btn-outline-primary active"> <span>{{ page_number }} <span class="sr-only">(current)</span></span> </button> {% else %} <a href="?page={{ page_number }}" class="btn btn-outline-primary"> {{ page_number }} </a> {% endif %} {% endfor %} {% if items_page.has_next %} <a href="?page={{ items_page.next_page_number }}" class="btn btn-outline-primary">»</a> {% endif %} </div> {% endif %}
{% else %} <p>No items found.</p>
{% endif %}
